body: The Jakarta Composite Index rose 0.95% or 69.47 points to 7,382.79, driven by stocks owned by Prajogo Pangestu, with PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (BREN) leading the gain, contributing 37.22 index points and surging 13.33% in stock price. Foreign investors recorded a net sell of IDR 170.36 billion, despite net buying 15.88 million BREN shares, 6.55 million PT Barito Pacific Tbk (BRPT) shares, and net selling 2.01 million PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA) shares.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Rises 0.95% Led by Prajogo Pangestu's Stocks

body: Foreign investors in Indonesia purchased 2.76 billion shares, with top buys including PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (151.94 million shares), PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (1.41 billion shares), and PT Surya Citra Media Tbk (62.10 million shares), resulting in a net foreign volume of -45.38 million shares. Meanwhile, top sells included PT Bukalapak.com Tbk (-65.24 million shares), PT Bumi Resources Tbk (-41.37 million shares), and PT Wir Asia Tbk (-32.55 million shares), among others.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia's stock market show mixed sentiment, with net sales of 45.38 million shares on Friday, December 6.

body: Barito Group's five companies, including PT Barito Renewables Energy TBK (BREN), PT Chandra Asri Pacific TBK (TPIA), and PT Petrosea TBK (PTRO), have a combined market capitalization of IDR 1.91 quadrillion, representing 15.96% of the total market capitalization of approximately IDR 12 quadrillion. Among these companies, PTRO saw a 342.73% stock surge, TPIA a 41.66% increase, and BREN an 8.55% rise, while BRPT and CUAN experienced stock declines of 29.81% and 38.01%, respectively.
title: Barito Group's Stocks Show Mixed Performance in Capital Market with Three Companies Recording Positive Growth.

body: Anderson Bay Pte Ltd (no ticker) has acquired a 10% stake in PT Exploitasi Energi Indonesia Tbk (CNKO) for IDR 6.27 billion, purchasing 896 million shares at IDR 7 per share, as CNKO seeks to address its capital deficiency issues. CNKO reported a capital deficiency of IDR 1.23 trillion as of September 30, 2024, which has severely impacted its stock performance, with its share price plummeting 86% from IDR 50 to IDR 9 per share.
title: Anderson Bay Acquires 10% Stake in PT Exploitasi Energi Indonesia to Help Resolve Capital Deficiency Issues

body: Foreign investors bought and sold nearly similar amounts of shares on Thursday, December 5, resulting in a net foreign volume of 173.32 million shares, with top purchases including PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (227.64 million shares) and PT Bumi Resources Tbk (134.69 million shares). Meanwhile, top sales included PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (-84.66 million shares), PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (-24.07 million shares), and PT Avia Avian Tbk (-41.46 million shares).
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia buy and sell shares in nearly equal amounts, with energy and property companies among the most purchased.

body: PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk (DSSA) reported a net profit of USD 243.85 million in Q3 2024, a 34.41% year-over-year decline from USD 371.79 million in Q3 2023, driven by a 45.22% drop in revenue to USD 2.24 billion. The company's gross profit and operating profit also fell 45.35% and 48.73%, respectively, with significant declines in revenue contributions from its coal mining trading, trading, and steam power supply segments.
title: PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa's net profit and revenue decline significantly in Q3 2024 due to poor performance in coal mining and trading segments.

body: PT Bank Amar Indonesia Tbk (AMAR) has launched its embedded banking service, targeting the B2B segment, to empower micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) with seamless financial management, offering services like account opening and credit distribution directly from partner platforms. The bank aims to expand its partnerships across diverse industries by 2025-2026, mitigating risks with customized credit disbursement and repayment schedules tailored to each industry's unique cash flow cycles.
title: Amar Bank Launches Embedded Banking Service to Empower Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises in Indonesia
